title: Pre Instrumentality Shinjis Delusion or Actual Occurrence
display_title: Pre Instrumentality Shinjis Delusion or Actual Occurrence
wikitext:
[]: Pre-Instrumentality: Shinji's Delusion or Actual Occurrence?             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:07 pm [Split from the Fanfic Recommendations thread. - Monk] Chuckman wrote: He had a pretty intense reaction to it, but his decision to tang everyone came after she rejected him in pre-Instrumentality. He did it because she turned him away.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:12 pm SEELE wrote: Nah, she didn't turn him away. That was he himself. He thought about Asuka in that way. He was nearly insane at the moment. It was a hallucination. And that is why he strangled Asuka at the beach in the first place and nearly breakdown as she caress his face. The conversation is a mirror to what he said to the comatose Asuka. And he rewinded everything even with the same sentences through Asuka. Pre-Instrumentality is the judging mind of Shinji. He admits to himself "even if open up to Asuka she would turn me away". In the context what GNR said before she releases the AT. Fields make that way even more sense. Its like the sandbox. A hallucination. Asukas kimochi warui is a reference to the masturbation, the tears on her cheek and the crying boy on top of her.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:18 pm SEELE wrote: In EoE? Really? If that is the case ... i should rewrite my AU with Asuka "dreams" about this timeline. If Unit 01 didn't move til Unit 02 was shred apart then Yui is a fucked up plot device.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:22 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: Actually everyone in Pre Instrumentality was their real selves. I don't think the "This is the Asuka of Shinji's mind" or vice versa crap was started til everyone was thrown into the Red Sea.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:24 pm SEELE wrote: Just look at the script.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:31 pm Personally, I'd say Yui budged when EVa 01's angelic spider senses told her all the other impact ingredients were assembled, and no earlier. Not sure how much she even knew/percieved of what was physically going on on the surface, but she might've been calculating that everyone involved would get their asses saved by their imminent tangification either way. ...Being eviscerated is still an experience/memory poor Asuka could have done without, tho. As if she wasn't traumatized enough already.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:34 pm Asuka in Pre-Instrumentality was absolutely her real self, and a close reading of the scene shows that the apartment is her personal hell, akin to Shinji's hell train, and he was an intruder in her mind-space. She was upset with him for barging into her limbo and demanding that she stroke his ego after he directed at an insult at her by jerking off over her body, something so calculated to throw her over the edge and make her hate him it's actually worse than if he'd simply raped her. All of this has been discussed previously, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:42 pm Chuckman wrote: Asuka in Pre-Instrumentality was absolutely her real self, and a close reading of the scene shows that the apartment is her personal hell, akin to Shinji's hell train, and he was an intruder in her mind-space. She was upset with him for barging into her limbo and demanding that she stroke his ego after he directed at an insult at her by jerking off over her body, something so calculated to throw her over the edge and make her hate him it's actually worse than if he'd simply raped her. All of this has been discussed previously, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:43 pm Note that only the "dead" people showed up in pre-instrumentality, that Shinji saw things he shouldn't have had acess too if those were just their representations in his mind (Misato's college fuck vacation with Kaji), and that EVA 01 had already transformed into the tree of life ("source of all souls" and possible "ark", according to Fuyu). See also Misato reacting to hearing of their smoochie experiment under her room. "No, you're too young for kissing! ...Grat, now they're devirginifying each other under my roof. I'm a bad mom " That was definitely them- the first sould beginning to melt into each other as they get collected in the ToL. I don't think Shinji was necessarily doing any concious intruding her, this was before he chose to go for the red button, rather, a mind is a terrible thing to read and having them melt into each other is naturally ugly, especially if there's a lot of undischarged feelings between you and the other meltees. It makes sense that before they truly merge, they'd each learn just what exactly the other thinks about them, which, in Asuka's case, includes being understandably pissed.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 4:51 pm Kendrix wrote: Personally, I'd say Yui budged when EVa 01's angelic spider senses told her all the other impact ingredients were assembled, and no earlier. Not sure how much she even knew/percieved of what was physically going on on the surface, but she might've been calculating that everyone involved would get their asses saved by their imminent tangification either way.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Mon Apr 21, 2014 6:43 pm SEELE wrote: If that is the case. Why did she even caress his face? Begging for her life? This doesn't even make any sense. She could have easily slaps him. And this "way of thinking" brings me to the point where i have already been. There is no A/S salution. Im relieved that you guys thought me a lesson. Im outta here.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:11 pm This is true. I do know that the implication behind the line was that there was some connection to what he had done (Anno basically told the actress 'Imagine you learned someone did this over your unconscious body.'). My personal interpretation is that there was a combination of acceptance (having seen/felt/understood the sensations that drove him to that) and disgust (she, herself, reacting to all of that, both the act itself, the knowledge of it, and the self-loathing that drove Shinji to do it). Working with a foundation like that, you have a literal two-way street: who knows what comes from this point? The foundation is set for either forgiveness or resentment. Which, in a way, almost shows the futility of Instrumentality. Perhaps the return of people, instead, leads to resentment. Perhaps Instrumentality is impossible because, instead of a merging of memories and identities, humanity clings harder and harder to themselves, shocked and dismayed at the baggage others held, and how that connects back to them.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:16 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: My personal interpretation is that there was a combination of acceptance (having seen/felt/understood the sensations that drove him to that) and disgust (she, herself, reacting to all of that, both the act itself, the knowledge of it, and the self-loathing that drove Shinji to do it). Working with a foundation like that, you have a literal two-way street: who knows what comes from this point? The foundation is set for either forgiveness or resentment.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:24 pm "I love you! I hate you! I love you! I hate you!"

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:27 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: "I love you! I hate you! I love you! I hate you!"

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:29 pm Nobody can love another person without acknowledging their flaws.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:30 pm Why would Asuka suddenly refer back to the jerking off apropos of nothing? Kind of a lot had happened in between. That notion always struck me as weird. Unless in fact from her point of view this is the first time she's met Shinji since that event. (Kidding! Sufficient evidence for the fact that P3II was a legitimate mind-meld is found in the fact that Shinji was shown therein things he did not already know about, like Kaji and Misato's week of sex.)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:35 pm Monk Ed wrote: Why would Asuka suddenly refer back to the jerking off apropos of nothing? Kind of a lot had happened in between. That notion always struck me as weird.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:40 pm I can always imagine the conversation the next day... A: "...I still can't believe you did that." S: "Oh, come on! When will you let me live that down?" A: "How about never? Do you think never might work for you!?" S: "The world...is over! This is after the end of the world!" A: "You have your priorities, I have mine!"

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Apr 21, 2014 8:44 pm Bagheera wrote: But the a lot had little to do with them. It all makes sense in context.
